source_intake_record.raw_question_text: Cyclopropane, pictured above, is a relatively unstable compound. As seen in the diagram, the carbon atoms form the corners of an equilateral triangle and each carbon atom has two hydrogen atoms attached to complete an octet of electrons around the carbon atoms. Based upon this structure, why is cyclopropane a relatively unstable compound?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text: The bonds do not match the angles.

choice_map.A: Hydrocarbon compounds are relatively unstable in general.
choice_map.B: Compounds that have identical atoms bonded to each other are relatively unstable.
choice_map.C: The bonds do not match the angles.
choice_map.D: There is no resonance to stabilize the compound.

image field contains an in-memory PIL image representation, not a path or URL
